game_theme.title: Angles, Lines, and Shape Classification
game_theme.mission_intro: Use points, lines, rays, angles, line relationships, symmetry, and shape attributes to help the Skill World crew design a safe city plaza.

lesson.story_mission: The crew must label paths, corners, signs, and shapes in a city plaza.
lesson.mini_lesson: Geometry uses exact words. Angles are made by rays with a shared vertex, protractors measure degrees, parallel lines never meet, perpendicular lines make right angles, and symmetry means matching halves.
lesson.objective: Identify and classify angles, lines, line relationships, symmetry, and shapes.
lesson.concept_explanation: Grade 4 geometry connects diagrams to vocabulary. The marks, endpoints, angle openings, and side relationships show how to classify each figure.
lesson.common_misconception: Students may judge angles by ray length, read the wrong protractor scale, confuse parallel and perpendicular lines, or choose a fold line that does not make matching halves.
lesson.real_world_connection: These skills help with maps, architecture, art, robotics paths, and construction plans.
lesson.lesson_summary: Name the parts of the figure, use measurement when needed, and classify by attributes instead of appearance.

audio.text: In this lesson, use geometry vocabulary to read angles, line relationships, symmetry, and shape attributes precisely.
audio.label: Listen to Lesson

misconception_bank.angle_size_confusion: Judges angle size by ray length or picture size instead of opening.
misconception_bank.protractor_reading_error: Reads the wrong protractor scale or misplaces the vertex.
misconception_bank.parallel_perpendicular_confusion: Confuses lines that never meet with lines that meet at right angles.
misconception_bank.symmetry_line_error: Chooses a fold line that does not make matching halves.

story.text: Read this page to enter Angles, Lines, and Shape Classification. The crew has a real Grade 4 problem to solve, so notice the quantities, labels, and math words before you start. Think about what the question is asking and how a model can keep your work organized.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: Read this page for the main lesson on points, lines, rays, angles, perpendicular and parallel lines, symmetry, and shape classification. Use precise vocabulary, name the units or geometry attributes, and explain why each step makes sense. If a number, line, angle, or shape label matters, say it in your own words before answering.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Read this page while you watch the worked example. The model is not decoration; it shows how to connect the problem words to a mathematical relationship. Pause on each label and ask what it tells you to calculate, compare, measure, or classify.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: Read this page before the demo. Follow the first move, then predict the next move before checking the example. Good mathematicians use the visual evidence and the answer sentence together, not just a quick guess.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Read this page to practice with support. Press Read Question for each problem so you hear the full math wording clearly. Then use the model, choose or type your answer, and check whether your response includes the correct value, unit, or geometry word.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Read this page for the challenge round. These problems may mix vocabulary, diagrams, and computation, so slow down and identify the exact relationship first. Press Read Question when you need the prompt spoken again, then explain your reasoning.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Read this page for the checkpoint. This is your chance to show independent skill with the same careful habits: read the prompt, use the visual, answer with the correct label, and review your work. Use Read Question to hear each checkpoint item.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Read this page to celebrate your progress. The badge means you practiced the lesson, watched models, tried challenges, and checked your understanding. Name one strategy you used today so it is ready for the next math mission.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: Read this page to save your growth profile. Look at what you completed and notice which kinds of questions felt strongest. Your next practice should build on those strengths and revisit any unit labels, data labels, angle measures, or shape attributes that still need attention.
profile.label: Read This Page
